blocks.body: Relative pronouns are pronouns that connect parts of a complex sentence and give additional information about the noun. Basic: who (for people), which (for things), that (universal), whose (whose), whom (whom - formally).

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Who — for people (subject/appendix): The man who called...", "Which — for things and animals: The book which I read...", "That — universal (people/things), but NOT after commas!", "Whose — whose (belonging): The man whose car...", "Whom — whom (formally, instead of who as an addition).", "Defining clauses — without commas (important information). You can that.", "Non-defining clauses — with commas (additional information). Only who/which!", "After best, all, every, only, first it is better to use that." ] }
